topic: A student learns their scholarship application was denied
raw_output:
[story 1]

The rejection email sat on the desk, stark white against the dark wood. Elias stared at the screen, his fingers hovering over the mouse, unwilling to click. The silence of his small apartment was oppressive, amplifying the hollow thudding in his chest. He traced the outline of the rejection letter with his thumb, the crisp paper feeling alien and accusatory. Every corner of the room seemed too sharp, the shadows stretching long and distorted under the weak desk lamp. He didn't move to turn off the light; the darkness felt safer, a blank canvas where the failure couldn't be seen. A slow, ragged exhale escaped him, a sound swallowed instantly by the quiet room.

[story 2]

"Denied." The single word echoed in the cavern of his mind, a dull, heavy stone settling in his gut. Maya leaned back in her chair, the cheap plastic groaning in protest. She picked at a loose thread on her worn sweater, her gaze fixed on the condensation tracking down her water glass. Outside, the city hummed with indifferent energy, a soundtrack to her profound stillness. She picked up her pen, but the ink refused to flow. Her jaw was tight, the muscles in her neck corded with tension. She just watched the dust motes dance in the single shaft of sunlight piercing the blinds, feeling utterly anchored to the floor, unable to summon the energy to even lift her head.

[story 3]

He paced the length of his cramped dorm room, the worn carpet muffling the sound of his restless steps. Each footfall seemed to scrape against the silence, a desperate attempt to displace the suffocating weight in his chest. His hands clenched into fists at his sides, the knuckles turning white against his skin. He stopped abruptly by the window, pressing his forehead against the cool glass. The view of the campus below seemed impossibly distant, a world he was no longer invited to join. A low, almost inaudible sound escaped his throat—a sound of deep, internal frustration—before he spun around, unable to settle, trapped in a loop of heavy, unproductive motion.
